tasks.title: Implement Centralized Authentication and RBAC for API Endpoints
tasks.description: Implement a robust authentication mechanism (e.g., JWT, OAuth2) and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) across all sensitive API endpoints. This is critical to prevent unauthorized access to administrative and internal functionalities.
subtasks: Research and select an Identity Provider (IdP) or implement a standalone JWT issuance/validation service.
Develop JWT validation middleware for all services (Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance).
Define roles (e.g., admin, crew, finance, customer) and their corresponding permissions.
Implement RBAC checks at the API endpoint level for all sensitive operations (e.g., POST /api/v1/catalog/products, PATCH /api/v1/catalog/products/:id, all RMS and Finance endpoints).
Integrate authentication and authorization into the API gateway (if applicable) or directly into each service.
Develop unit and integration tests for authentication and RBAC logic.
Conduct penetration testing against all protected endpoints to verify access controls.
acceptance_criteria: All sensitive API endpoints require a valid, authenticated token.
Users with insufficient roles are denied access to protected resources (403 Forbidden).
JWT tokens are correctly validated for signature, expiry, and claims.
No unauthorized access is possible to admin product creation, invoice management, crew scheduling, or other internal operations.
Penetration test report confirms no bypasses for authentication or authorization.

tasks.title: Secure Infrastructure Credentials using Kubernetes Secrets
tasks.description: Replace the use of ConfigMaps for storing sensitive infrastructure credentials (PostgreSQL, Redis, S3/R2) with Kubernetes Secrets. For production, consider integrating a dedicated secret management solution.
subtasks: Modify Task 1's implementation to create Kubernetes Secrets for PostgreSQL, Redis, and S3/R2 credentials instead of a ConfigMap.
Update all affected services (Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance) to consume credentials from Kubernetes Secrets.
Implement a strategy for rotating these secrets.
(Optional, for future) Research and propose integration with External Secrets Operator or a cloud KMS for enhanced secret management.
acceptance_criteria: No sensitive credentials are stored in plaintext ConfigMaps.
All services successfully connect to their respective databases and storage using credentials from Kubernetes Secrets.
Secrets are managed and rotated securely.

tasks.title: Implement Secure File Upload Validation for S3/R2
tasks.description: Enhance the S3/R2 file upload mechanism in the Equipment Catalog service to include strict input validation, preventing malicious file hosting, denial of service, and other upload-related vulnerabilities.
subtasks: Implement server-side validation for uploaded image files: Strict MIME type checking (e.g., image/jpeg, image/png).
Enforce maximum file size limits (e.g., 5MB).
Sanitize file names, or preferably, generate unique, non-guessable file names (e.g., UUIDs) for storage.
Ensure images are served with appropriate Content-Type headers.
Add unit and integration tests for the upload validation logic.
acceptance_criteria: Only allowed image MIME types (JPEG, PNG) can be uploaded.
Files exceeding the maximum size limit are rejected.
Uploaded file names are sanitized or randomized to prevent path traversal or malicious execution.
No malicious files can be uploaded or served via the S3/R2 integration.

tasks.title: Enforce Pod Security Standards and Secure securityContexts
tasks.description: Implement and enforce Kubernetes Pod Security Standards (PSS) at the namespace level and configure secure securityContexts for all deployed services to ensure least-privilege execution.
subtasks: Apply the restricted Pod Security Standard to the sigma1 namespace (and databases if applicable for operators).

Review all service deployment manifests (Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance) to include securityContext configurations: runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, capabilities: drop: ["ALL"].
Verify that services still function correctly after applying these security contexts.
Add automated checks (e.g., OPA Gatekeeper policies) to enforce PSS and securityContext configurations in CI/CD.
acceptance_criteria: sigma1 namespace (and databases) enforces the restricted PSS profile.
All deployed pods run as non-root users.
All deployed pods have readOnlyRootFilesystem enabled where appropriate.
All deployed pods drop unnecessary capabilities.
No pods are deployed with privileged escalation.
Automated checks prevent deployment of non-compliant pods.

tasks.title: Implement Security Audit Logging for Critical Actions
tasks.description: Introduce structured security audit logging for all state-mutating API calls, especially within the Finance service, to ensure traceability and compliance.
subtasks: Identify all state-mutating API endpoints in the Finance service (e.g., POST /api/v1/invoices, POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/send, POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/paid).
Implement structured audit logging for these endpoints, capturing: Authenticated user ID (if authentication is implemented by Task 7).
Action performed (e.g., "invoice_created", "invoice_status_updated").
Timestamp.
Resource ID (e.g., invoice_id).
Relevant parameters or changes.
Ensure audit logs are immutable, securely aggregated, and retained.
Extend audit logging to other critical services (e.g., Equipment Catalog product creation/updates, RMS project status changes) as appropriate.
Develop tests to verify audit logs are correctly generated and contain required information.
acceptance_criteria: All state-mutating actions in the Finance service generate a structured audit log entry.
Audit logs contain user ID, action, timestamp, and resource ID.
Audit logs are securely stored and accessible for review.
No critical actions can be performed without an associated audit trail.
